assignment: Independently replay and challenge the exact session deactivation contract. Verify all ten cases and the preserved six activation behaviors, then decide the contract separately from the current product wall breach.
required_checks: Run the saved cleanup replay and verify every cited hash.
Challenge whether activation-local cancelled settlement is safely distinct from durable business-state mutation.
Challenge abort-all-before-wait, join-all, idempotency, error aggregation, checkpoint, finish, and both race orders.
Inspect current Runtime and Lite cleanup ordering to confirm the proposed placement is SDK-native and needs no Lite primitive.
Audit the checkpoint against the executable DKR contract and every active wall.
State whether verified discovery can authorize a narrowly scoped remediation despite the current baseline wall breach under the canonical skill; do not invent or silently weaken a rule.
Run store verification after governed output.
forbidden: Product code, tests, docs, manifests, git history, PR state, external services, frame, tree, or another worker's artifacts
Self-certification, a Lite primitive, ownership change, hidden cleanup effect, or wall weakening
required_outputs: workers/validator-dkr-1-cleanup/progress.jsonl via the absolute OKRA store helper worker-report command
terminal_condition: Finish with an explicit verified, rejected, or blocked disposition, exact wall reads, and a governed progress record.
